status: SAMPLE — illustrative format for data/tag_vocab.json. Copy this to data/tag_vocab.json (git-ignored) and edit for your consumer. sjv reads only fields[*].values (hard enum) and fields[*].cardinality (soft).
purpose: The single controlled-vocabulary config for registry ontology tags. One entry per FIELD (axis), each with acceptable `values`, a soft `cardinality` expectation, and per-value `glosses` (legend).
contract: FIELD SET = the keys under `fields` (adding an axis is an edit here, not an sjv schema change). HARD: sjv rejects any ontology value not in its field's `values`, or any populated field not in the vocab. SOFT: `cardinality` is an expectation only — never rejected; a missing required axis is surfaced by view('anomalies'). Values alphabetized. A bare `{field: [values]}` map (no `fields` wrapper, no metadata) is also accepted.

object.gloss: what the declaration is ABOUT. Replace these placeholder values with your own.

glosses.example-noun: PLACEHOLDER — a thing the declaration is about.
glosses.example-verb: PLACEHOLDER — an act the declaration is about.

domain.gloss: which field the declaration lives in. Replace these placeholder values with your own.

role.gloss: the declaration's structural function. These values mirror the fixed sjv role enum.

glosses.bridge: a transform connecting two domains — an edge, not a cell.
glosses.commitment: a labeled modeling commitment / identity claim; held, not proved.
glosses.face: an (object, domain) cell — an object realized inside one domain.
glosses.no-go: an apophatic / obstruction result — establishes what something is NOT (counterexample, no-morphism, negation with no fixed point).
glosses.scaffolding: a helper / stepping-stone declaration, not a headline claim.
glosses.schema: structure about the registry itself — the synthesis / meta layer.
